Competitor Tracker sends you a digest email every Monday. But agents get a webhook, so you do not need to give them access to your inbox. Same data, same filters, same time. Finally, the website is built for humans and agents. We serve markdown for agents and HTML for humans. If you want to test it out, there’s a switch at the bottom of every page that helps you enter the Matrix. Pardon, it helps you see the “AI agent” view. Besides humans and agents, we also have a dog - C. T. Lucky is our lead detective who guides you through our new noir platform and sends you Monday “dossiers.” It’s an AI dog, but still a good one. So, it’s time - meet Competitor Tracker & Co.: the AI agency whose pack of agents tails your competitors’ public pages all week and files one short, ranked dossier every Monday, so your team reads the moves that matter instead of babysitting another alert feed - https://competitortracker.io. If you want to meet C. T. Lucky and Competitor Tracker & Co., head to the “What is Competitor Tracker & Co.” page, see what our pack of agents can do, or check out the product demo (for humans and agents) and try it out (it takes a few minutes to set it up, and the first 25 coins are on the house). Building Competitor Tracker I saw an amazing tweet yesterday: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381. It’s almost perfectly aligned with my experience building products with AI so far. You need an idea and a few hours for a breathtaking demo. Then you need months to finish the last 10%. Of course, that’s not a real 10%, it’s an illusion that tricks you into thinking you can do everything almost instantly. I would go a step further and say that you need a few hours for a demo, a few weeks to implement 200% of the code, and then months to clean up all the AI slop and replace a small part of it with working features. Image based on: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381. Something similar happened to Competitor Tracker. But that wasn’t the fun part of the journey. Personally, the fun part was setting everything up. And everything started with a brand decision. I tried out a few styles, and the film noir one looked like the best fit. At the same time, I asked Claude Code to prepare the initial set of documents based on the prototype source code. Then, because I know that Lav will connect his Hermes and OpenClaw agents to the repo, I decided to separate the product and marketing website repositories. Don’t get me wrong, I like both Hermes and OpenClaw, but I do not trust any AI agent with the full set of permissions. The next step was picking up the platform. AWS was an obvious choice, as I know it well enough. But obvious choices are not fun. To make the process more fun and learn something new, I picked Cloudflare instead. Why Cloudflare? I wanted to try it out for a long time. I know a few smart people who work there, and some other smart people I respect praised it. That was already a good excuse to try it out. However, there’s also another, probably even more important perspective: AWS was too low-level for this application, and Cloudflare has most of the critical pieces as services: Workers for compute, Browser Run for scraping, AI Gateway for accessing AI agents, R2 for file storage, and Email service for sending weekly emails. All boxes checked. The best of all was being able to calculate the exact cost of the system on a single platform and decide on the pricing for Competitor Tracker. Cloudflare has its downsides, too. AWS is superior in many areas, including fine-grained permissions, monitoring, sub-accounts (staging, etc.), and more. E.g., AWS is a way better platform for our other product - Vacation Tracker. But Cloudflare still seems like a perfect fit for Competitor Tracker. After selecting the platform, setting up tooling, a CI/CD pipeline, and a staging environment, and configuring all critical verifications and tests, it was time to onboard Lav’s agents. And then, the magic started happening. Claude Code was in charge of features. Open Code with GLM-5.2 joined as a collaborator (and impressed me as I didn’t expect much). ChatGPT helped with images and to stay on brand. Hermes and OpenClaw started filling in the marketing website’s missing pieces and occasionally even worked on the product. We finally migrated all the existing users to a new platform almost a month ago. And we had our first sign-up from someone we do not know! We also invited a few more friends, gathered some feedback, and made a few improvements to the platform. Then, we went on vacation. Well, it’s important to rest, probably even more important now with AI, because you can accomplish more when you are focused. But now that vacation is over, it’s time to continue. The product seems stable enough. A limited number of users are onboarded, and we already track more than 2,000 companies! The goal is to track more than 130 industries and build quarterly industry overview pages. You can see the first ~30 of them on our website. It’s time to focus on the marketing. But before we do that, let’s talk about the architecture and how we set everything up. Architecture As I already explained, we picked Cloudflare this time. We could use a mix of AWS and Cloudflare to get the best of both worlds, but I prefer having a single platform for most of the infrastructure. You can call that vendor lock-in if you’d like (but our AWS bill of 1-1.5% of Vacation Tracker’s MRR after 8 years does not really care), or a single point of failure (but when Cloudflare or AWS are down, you probably have more significant problems than not being able to access the Competitor Tracker API). Still, these are all tradeoffs that we are willing to make. You can also argue that this could be a much simpler app on a Hetzner instance, but I still have PTSD from years of figuring out why the app crashed at 10 PM, and this is also more fun anyway. An oversimplified architecture would look like the following diagram. In short: The Competitor Tracker app treats humans and AI agents as equal users. Humans can use either the web dashboard or an API. Or they can simply talk to their favorite AI agents. AI Agents use the API or the MCP. Our marketing website is created for both humans and agents. We serve HTML to humans and Markdown to AI agents (based on the browser user agent). You can also test both views by switching from the “In person” (human) view to the “By wire” (AI agent) view. The Web dashboard is a React application served via Cloudflare Pages. There’s nothing especially interesting there. MCP is a Node.js application that runs in a Cloudflare Worker. It supports all auth types (OAuth2 and API key) and all cool things we found. Both the web dashboard and MCP use the Competitor Tracker API. Everything you do in Competitor Tracker you can do using our API. The public one. We also run multiple AI agents that work on a marketing website and use the API to fill in the data. Of course, agents work on the app, too. I personally wrote up to 3 lines of code. It was faster than explaining to my coding agent what to change. API is another Node.js app in a Cloudflare Worker. It uses the OpenAPI spec, which also generates up-to-date API docs with each deployment. The API docs worker is not in the diagram, but it uses fumadocs, and it’s built as a static app (I am not a fan of SSR React apps). The API requires either a bearer token or an API key. A dedicated auth worker is the identity provider for all apps: a self-hosted OAuth 2.0/OIDC server with passwordless email login and PKCE-secured authorization-code flow, issuing short-lived ES256 JWTs with rotating refresh tokens. It supports Dynamic Client Registration and standard discovery, so clients like the MCP server can register themselves and validate tokens against its JWKS with zero manual setup. The core of the system is another Cloudflare Worker that hosts the scraper. The scraper runs on a schedule and uses Cloudflare Browser Run and a few backup systems to convert all websites into HTML snapshots, screenshots, and markdown files. This is the most complex part of the system, as it handles the business logic, AI interactions, data storage, and all workflows with retries and self-recovery mechanisms. As already mentioned, the scraper uses multiple AI models to detect changes and filter out the noise. We decided to use the Cloudflare AI Gateway for everything so we can easily calculate the cost of each competitor we track. All pages (HTML and markdown files, and screenshots) are stored in the archive (Cloudflare R2). All changes and metadata are stored in the SQLite database (D1). Each Monday, another Cloudflare worker uses AI (via AI Gateway) to prepare Monday dossiers for humans and agents. Once the dossier is ready, humans receive emails on Monday morning (via Cloudflare Email service). At the same time, your AI agents receive the same dossier via webhook event. Of course, there’s much more than this. We needed observability, alerts, fallbacks, Slack integration, CI/CD, multiple coding agents, etc. How things work The idea is simple: outsource as much as possible to AI agents while still maintaining control over output quality. Or, to get back to our bottlenecks story from the beginning of this article: optimize the flow around our time, focus, and taste to maximize that local bottleneck as much as possible. What does that mean in practice? Many things, but the most important is the ability to verify the output of all our AI agents (coding and others) as much as possible before we review it. Ok, that seems logical, but how do you do that? By adding another LLM to check the output? No, not really. LLMs are not deterministic, which means that asking it the same question 10 times yields (often many) different answers. And that’s the main reason why they feel magical, and it’s also the main reason why you can’t just trust them. However, adding another LLM (even a different one) to verify the first LLM’s output does not fix the problem. It probably helps in many cases, but not in 100% of the cases. And when you know there’s a small chance that it can generate some weird output that can hurt your business, you need to keep your attention on 100% to be able to catch that if or when it happens. However, there’s one powerful tool that we use every day that can convert some of these checks to deterministic ones - a linter. When you write your code, you often have formatters (e.g., prettier, or in our case oxfmt) and linters (e.g., eslint, or in our case oxlint) that format your code and check if it follows the rules. Luckily, most of these formatters and linters are extendable - you can create a JavaScript plugin that checks your specific thing. But writing these plugins is not always easy and pleasant. But, again, luckily, your AI coding agent is quite good at writing these. With these deterministic checks, we can verify expected outcomes, such as whether our code follows the architecture guidelines (e.g., hexagonal architecture), whether migrations are destructive (we use deprecate-then-drop), and whether CSP is up to date. However, we also do deterministic checks for less common things, such as the voice of the application (we check for some specific words, and ban them, e.g., “harness,” that one is my favorite), or if our developer documentation leaks the implementation details (e.g., Cloudflare Workers, D1 database, R2, etc.). These deterministic checks catch many problematic issues before they reach our review. We also run them as part of the CI/CD pipeline, so we can let agents do more, since they know how to recover. On recovery, we also try to make linter errors as descriptive as possible and include the instructions on what to do instead. For example, instead of saying “destructive migrations are not allowed,” we say “stop using X in a prior commit … then land this destructive migration in a later commit,” and, when applicable, tell the agent about the escape hatch. But why stop there? These error hints are useful for both AI agents and humans, so we also baked them into the product. Each product error also returns the resolution hint. In addition to deterministic checks, we build various tools for our agents. We have tools that gather data and generate specific pages, tools for videos, tools for SEO checks, etc. The goal of these tools is to save our time and ensure that the agents have all the correct information. Then we use our time to review the output and do things agents can’t. Another big benefit in the age of AI agents is having the whole product in a monorepo. Your agent can verify features and capabilities before describing them on the product page, etc. You can also ask them to provide screenshots or even videos as part of the task to visually verify their work before running everything. Finally, you can also benefit from the specific cron jobs and background tasks, or, as they call them today, loops. For example, if you report the issue via our dashboard or an API (everything is an API, remember), it goes to our Linear board. Once Lav or I review these issues, we add additional instructions and the “autofix” label. Then our AI agents pick these up in the background, fix them, make a pull request, and provide screenshots or videos. We use Linear for issues and an amazing new product called Votito for product feedback and surveys. Votito allows you to score ideas from the idea bank using the Impact x Confidence x Ease (ICE) score by uploading documents such as A/B tests, market data, and usability studies. One of the future experiments is to try to make another background process that checks Votito for all ideas with a score over, e.g., 75, and builds them. We would still need to be in the driver’s seat and provide some of these documents, but it would be more fun. Lessons learned It’s still too early for a definite list of lessons, but so far, the key learnings are: Deterministic checks save you a lot of time. They can be linter plugins, or Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks. It does not matter, as long as they work for your setup. Descriptive errors with resolution hints help your AI agents (and also your users) to recover without bugging you. Monorepos give AI agents additional superpowers. E.g., your marketing agent knows how the product works. This also works for humans. Build custom tools for your agents and your setup. They’ll speed you up, allow you to change agents, and give you better outputs. Build cron jobs and background processes that can do things that do not need your input. Your AI agents can prepare everything and send it to you for a review. Have fun, because building with AI is fun! And it seems that our CofounderGPT might be possible, but not in the way we first imagined. Or, as they say on Twitter lately: build your own harness and loops :D But also be careful, because there are real people using your product. Beyond having fun, it needs to solve real-world problems. No one likes using AI slop. What’s next The potential roadmap is long, but our next step is to validate our product-market fit. This will probably mean the focus will be on marketing rather than the product. That’s also fun, and we have many ideas we want to try. If we manage to automate Competitor Tracker enough, and if Vacation Tracker allows, we might build some other products, too. I’ll keep you posted. And for you? Have fun and build cool and useful things. And, if you have competitors, you know, track them. I know a guy, pardon, a dog detective who can help you.

blocks.html_en: Rapid changes are sometimes scary. While working with AI is fun, it can also cause stress because it’s impossible to keep up with all the changes. Opening Twitter in the morning can lead to a mini panic attack because you missed new models that are way better than yesterday’s ones, Anthropic announced new deadlines for using their mythical models, new prompts that build products in one shot, tools, benchmarks, etc. Everything seems to be moving so fast that you forget you are in a bubble. Not the one that will burst tomorrow, but a bubble of early adopters.

sentences.en: Rapid changes are sometimes scary.
sentences.zh: 快速的变化有时令人恐惧。

sentences.en: While working with AI is fun, it can also cause stress because it’s impossible to keep up with all the changes.
sentences.zh: 虽然使用人工智能很有趣，但它也会带来压力，因为不可能跟上所有的变化。

sentences.en: Opening Twitter in the morning can lead to a mini panic attack because you missed new models that are way better than yesterday’s ones, Anthropic announced new deadlines for using their mythical models, new prompts that build products in one shot, tools, benchmarks, etc. Everything seems to be moving so fast that you forget you are in a bubble.
sentences.zh: 早上打开 Twitter 可能会导致一场小型恐慌，因为你错过了比昨天更好的新模型，Anthropic 宣布了使用他们神话模型的新截止日期，一次性构建产品的新提示、工具、基准等。一切似乎都进展得如此之快，以至于你忘记了自己正处于泡沫之中。

sentences.en: Not the one that will burst tomorrow, but a bubble of early adopters.
sentences.zh: 不是明天就会破裂的泡沫，而是早期采用者的泡沫。

blocks.html_en: While this flood of daily updates often brings mostly noise, the effects, benefits, and downsides of AI and LLMs are real. They have already affected many things. Including changing the way we build products, especially software. One of the natural consequences of these changes is that now is probably the best time to build your own product. Not the easiest time. That does not exist. Building products is playing the game on hard mode.

sentences.en: While this flood of daily updates often brings mostly noise, the effects, benefits, and downsides of AI and LLMs are real.
sentences.zh: 虽然每天大量的更新通常会带来噪音，但人工智能和法学硕士的影响、优点和缺点是真实的。

sentences.en: They have already affected many things.
sentences.zh: 他们已经影响了很多事情。

sentences.en: Including changing the way we build products, especially software.
sentences.zh: 包括改变我们构建产品的方式，尤其是软件。

sentences.en: One of the natural consequences of these changes is that now is probably the best time to build your own product.
sentences.zh: 这些变化的自然后果之一是，现在可能是构建自己的产品的最佳时机。

sentences.en: Not the easiest time.
sentences.zh: 不是最容易的时候。

sentences.en: That does not exist.
sentences.zh: 那是不存在的。

sentences.en: Building products is playing the game on hard mode.
sentences.zh: 构建产品就是在困难模式下玩游戏。

blocks.html_en: Let me tell you a story of a product that my co-founder, Lav, and I recently built. It’s a bit weird to write a long-form text by hand while multiple agents write and publish the code and new pages for our marketing site, but not all stories can and should be told by AI.

sentences.en: Let me tell you a story of a product that my co-founder, Lav, and I recently built.
sentences.zh: 让我告诉你一个我和联合创始人 Lav 最近开发的产品的故事。

sentences.en: It’s a bit weird to write a long-form text by hand while multiple agents write and publish the code and new pages for our marketing site, but not all stories can and should be told by AI.
sentences.zh: 当多个代理为我们的营销网站编写和发布代码和新页面时，手动编写长文本有点奇怪，但并非所有故事都可以而且应该由人工智能讲述。

blocks.html_en: Did AI shift the bottleneck for building products?

sentences.en: Did AI shift the bottleneck for building products?
sentences.zh: 人工智能是否改变了产品制造的瓶颈？

blocks.html_en: As the amazing book The Goal told us many, many years ago: there’s one main limiting factor for all organizations and for building anything. The book introduces the Theory of Constraints (TOC), which explains that we can move as fast as our main limiting factor (the bottleneck) allows.

sentences.en: As the amazing book The Goal told us many, many years ago: there’s one main limiting factor for all organizations and for building anything.
sentences.zh: 正如一本令人惊叹的书《目标》在很多年前告诉我们的那样：对于所有组织和构建任何东西来说，都有一个主要限制因素。

sentences.en: The book introduces the Theory of Constraints (TOC), which explains that we can move as fast as our main limiting factor (the bottleneck) allows.
sentences.zh: 这本书介绍了约束理论（TOC），它解释了我们可以在主要限制因素（瓶颈）允许的范围内尽可能快地前进。

blocks.html_en: Building products has always been hard, and many things have been real bottlenecks. Starting from the idea, then your investment (time and money spent building the product), taste and organization (what to build and how), actual product building (software development, design, testing, etc.), to the whole go-to-market motion (who do you sell the product to and how do you find people that would actually pay for it). But only one of these is the bottleneck for each organization. E.g., some organizations can add developers, designers, or testers, remove items from the backlog, or change how they manage products and projects to ship features faster and speed up the production pipeline, and make their production local bottleneck less restrictive. However, even with that, go-to-market may still be a bottleneck for them. Or you might have limited time to just a few hours each week, and no budget to hire anyone else, and no way to free up more time.

sentences.en: Building products has always been hard, and many things have been real bottlenecks.
sentences.zh: 构建产品一直很困难，很多事情都是真正的瓶颈。

sentences.en: Starting from the idea, then your investment (time and money spent building the product), taste and organization (what to build and how), actual product building (software development, design, testing, etc.), to the whole go-to-market motion (who do you sell the product to and how do you find people that would actually pay for it).
sentences.zh: 从想法开始，然后是你的投资（构建产品所花费的时间和金钱）、品味和组织（构建什么以及如何构建）、实际的产品构建（软件开发、设计、测试等），到整个上市行动（你将产品卖给谁以及如何找到真正愿意付费的人）。

sentences.en: But only one of these is the bottleneck for each organization.
sentences.zh: 但其中只有一个是每个组织的瓶颈。

sentences.en: E.g., some organizations can add developers, designers, or testers, remove items from the backlog, or change how they manage products and projects to ship features faster and speed up the production pipeline, and make their production local bottleneck less restrictive.
sentences.zh: 例如，一些组织可以添加开发人员、设计人员或测试人员，从待办事项中删除项目，或者更改管理产品和项目的方式，以更快地交付功能并加快生产流程，并减少生产本地瓶颈的限制。

sentences.en: However, even with that, go-to-market may still be a bottleneck for them.
sentences.zh: 然而，即便如此，进入市场可能仍然是他们的瓶颈。

sentences.en: Or you might have limited time to just a few hours each week, and no budget to hire anyone else, and no way to free up more time.
sentences.zh: 或者您每周的时间可能有限，只有几个小时，没有预算雇用其他人，也没有办法腾出更多时间。

blocks.html_en: The actual bottleneck varies from organization to organization and from product to product. But, if we need to pick the most common one, it’s probably go-to-market. Building products is hard, but selling them - unless you are one of the lucky marketing and sales masters, it’s extremely hard.

sentences.en: The actual bottleneck varies from organization to organization and from product to product.
sentences.zh: 实际瓶颈因组织和产品而异。

sentences.en: But, if we need to pick the most common one, it’s probably go-to-market.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果我们需要选择最常见的一种，它可能是上市的。

sentences.en: Building products is hard, but selling them - unless you are one of the lucky marketing and sales masters, it’s extremely hard.
sentences.zh: 制造产品很难，但销售它们——除非你是幸运的营销和销售大师之一，否则这是极其困难的。

blocks.html_en: So, did AI shift the bottleneck?

sentences.en: So, did AI shift the bottleneck?
sentences.zh: 那么，AI是否突破了瓶颈呢？

blocks.html_en: The answer is probably yes, but depending on your bottleneck, that could be good or bad news. Let me explain!

sentences.en: The answer is probably yes, but depending on your bottleneck, that could be good or bad news.
sentences.zh: 答案可能是肯定的，但根据您的瓶颈，这可能是好消息，也可能是坏消息。

sentences.en: Let me explain!
sentences.zh: 让我解释一下！

blocks.html_en: If your actual bottleneck was a lack of time for development, I have good news for you! AI can help you write code, design the app, write marketing copy, and even test the app.

sentences.en: If your actual bottleneck was a lack of time for development, I have good news for you!
sentences.zh: 如果您的实际瓶颈是缺乏发展时间，那么我有个好消息给您！

sentences.en: AI can help you write code, design the app, write marketing copy, and even test the app.
sentences.zh: 人工智能可以帮助您编写代码、设计应用程序、编写营销文案，甚至测试应用程序。

blocks.html_en: But I also have bad news.

sentences.en: But I also have bad news.
sentences.zh: 但我也有坏消息。

blocks.html_en: One of my favorite laws is the now 30-year-old Tog’s Law of Commuting, which pairs with Tesler’s Law of Conservation of Complexity: “The time of a commute is fixed. Only the distance is variable.” To put it in current terms: if we can write code and ship features faster, we won’t stop with the same amount of code/features as before and go fishing. Instead, we’ll spend the same time either shipping more features or code, or building other things.

sentences.en: One of my favorite laws is the now 30-year-old Tog’s Law of Commuting, which pairs with Tesler’s Law of Conservation of Complexity: “The time of a commute is fixed.
sentences.zh: 我最喜欢的定律之一是已有 30 年历史的托格通勤定律，它与特斯勒复杂性守恒定律相结合：“通勤时间是固定的。

sentences.en: Only the distance is variable.”
sentences.zh: 只是距离是可变的。”

sentences.en: To put it in current terms: if we can write code and ship features faster, we won’t stop with the same amount of code/features as before and go fishing.
sentences.zh: 用当前的话来说：如果我们能够更快地编写代码和发布功能，我们就不会像以前一样停止使用相同数量的代码/功能并去钓鱼。

sentences.en: Instead, we’ll spend the same time either shipping more features or code, or building other things.
sentences.zh: 相反，我们将花费相同的时间来交付更多功能或代码，或者构建其他东西。

blocks.html_en: That’s not bad news - you can do more by yourself! But the bad news is that others can too. So, the go-to-market strategy becomes an even bigger bottleneck, as it’s hard to reach the right potential customers and cut through all the noise and actual competitors.

sentences.en: That’s not bad news - you can do more by yourself!
sentences.zh: 这不是坏消息 - 您可以自己做更多事情！

sentences.en: But the bad news is that others can too.
sentences.zh: 但坏消息是其他人也可以。

sentences.en: So, the go-to-market strategy becomes an even bigger bottleneck, as it’s hard to reach the right potential customers and cut through all the noise and actual competitors.
sentences.zh: 因此，进入市场策略成为一个更大的瓶颈，因为很难接触到合适的潜在客户并消除所有噪音和实际竞争对手。

blocks.html_en: Ok, but building products is faster now, right? If that guy on Twitter one-shotted the whole application using Claude Fable 5, everyone could do that, right? Well, not really. There’s a big difference between an amazing demo app and a product. But let me tell you our backstory first.

sentences.en: Ok, but building products is faster now, right?
sentences.zh: 好的，但是现在构建产品速度更快了，对吧？

sentences.en: If that guy on Twitter one-shotted the whole application using Claude Fable 5, everyone could do that, right?
sentences.zh: 如果 Twitter 上的那个人使用 Claude Fable 5 一次性完成了整个应用程序，那么每个人都可以做到这一点，对吧？

sentences.en: Well, not really.
sentences.zh: 嗯，不是真的。

sentences.en: There’s a big difference between an amazing demo app and a product.
sentences.zh: 令人惊叹的演示应用程序和产品之间有很大的区别。

sentences.en: But let me tell you our backstory first.
sentences.zh: 但让我先告诉你我们的背景故事。

blocks.html_en: Backstory

sentences.en: Backstory
sentences.zh: 背景故事

blocks.html_en: As mentioned in my previous article, “Employees, AI, and AI employees,” more than 3 years ago, my cofounder, Lav, and I tried to create an AI cofounder (CofounderGPT, as we called it), had a lot of fun, and failed. Well, to be honest, that’s not 100% true anymore, as we have had an AI agent called CofounderGPT working with us for the last 6 months, and it played a big role in building our new product, but we’ll talk about that later. In the meantime, if you are curious, check https://cofoundergpt.ai for CofounderGPT’s weekly log. But let’s get back to the “origin” story.

sentences.en: As mentioned in my previous article, “Employees, AI, and AI employees,” more than 3 years ago, my cofounder, Lav, and I tried to create an AI cofounder (CofounderGPT, as we called it), had a lot of fun, and failed.
sentences.zh: 正如我在上一篇文章《员工、AI 和 AI 员工》中提到的，三年多前，我和我的联合创始人 Lav 试图创建一个 AI 联合创始人（我们称之为 CoFounderGPT），玩得很开心，但失败了。

sentences.en: Well, to be honest, that’s not 100% true anymore, as we have had an AI agent called CofounderGPT working with us for the last 6 months, and it played a big role in building our new product, but we’ll talk about that later.
sentences.zh: 好吧，说实话，这不再是 100% 正确的了，因为在过去 6 个月里，我们有一个名为 CoFounderGPT 的 AI 代理与我们合作，它在构建我们的新产品方面发挥了重要作用，但我们稍后会讨论这一点。

sentences.en: In the meantime, if you are curious, check https://cofoundergpt.ai for CofounderGPT’s weekly log.
sentences.zh: 同时，如果您好奇，请查看 https://coFoundergpt.ai 了解 CoFounderGPT 的每周日志。

sentences.en: But let’s get back to the “origin” story.
sentences.zh: 但让我们回到“起源”的故事。

blocks.html_en: Everything started with our own AI experiments. However, because the time we could spend building fun stuff with AI was our main bottleneck at the time, we hired someone and started an “R&D department.” That was fun, as we managed to rebuild some of the cool demos from Twitter (and most of them were cool only as demos and magic tricks), try out different models, and even fine-tune a few.

sentences.en: Everything started with our own AI experiments.
sentences.zh: 一切都始于我们自己的人工智能实验。

sentences.en: However, because the time we could spend building fun stuff with AI was our main bottleneck at the time, we hired someone and started an “R&D department.”
sentences.zh: 然而，因为我们用人工智能构建有趣的东西的时间是我们当时的主要瓶颈，所以我们雇了一个人并成立了一个“研发部门”。

sentences.en: That was fun, as we managed to rebuild some of the cool demos from Twitter (and most of them were cool only as demos and magic tricks), try out different models, and even fine-tune a few.
sentences.zh: 这很有趣，因为我们设法重建了 Twitter 上的一些很酷的演示（其中大多数只是作为演示和魔术而很酷），尝试不同的模型，甚至微调了一些模型。

blocks.html_en: Finally, we started working on CofounderGPT and building a small product with AI. Both things failed because you can’t really outsource product building, especially not in an emerging market like LLMs. Also, we were not patient enough. We wanted better models, and, at that time, thought that we were already late.

sentences.en: Finally, we started working on CofounderGPT and building a small product with AI.
sentences.zh: 最后，我们开始在 CoFounderGPT 上工作，并用人工智能构建了一个小产品。

sentences.en: Both things failed because you can’t really outsource product building, especially not in an emerging market like LLMs.
sentences.zh: 这两件事都失败了，因为你无法真正外包产品构建，尤其是在像法学硕士这样的新兴市场。

sentences.en: Also, we were not patient enough.
sentences.zh: 另外，我们也没有足够的耐心。

sentences.en: We wanted better models, and, at that time, thought that we were already late.
sentences.zh: 我们想要更好的模型，但当时我们认为我们已经迟到了。

blocks.html_en: We still want to build a product with AI. But we needed to narrow it down. What if, instead of building the whole CofounderGPT, we built one small component? Something that would solve our problem, save our time, and give us better insights. There were many ideas, but one was focusing on an annoying problem that we had - someone needed to check our direct and indirect competitors’ websites, present the most important changes, and maintain that big and always outdated spreadsheet.

sentences.en: We still want to build a product with AI.
sentences.zh: 我们仍然想用人工智能来打造一个产品。

sentences.en: But we needed to narrow it down.
sentences.zh: 但我们需要缩小范围。

sentences.en: What if, instead of building the whole CofounderGPT, we built one small component?
sentences.zh: 如果我们不构建整个 CoFounderGPT，而是构建一个小组件，会怎样？

sentences.en: Something that would solve our problem, save our time, and give us better insights.
sentences.zh: 它可以解决我们的问题，节省我们的时间，并为我们提供更好的见解。

sentences.en: There were many ideas, but one was focusing on an annoying problem that we had - someone needed to check our direct and indirect competitors’ websites, present the most important changes, and maintain that big and always outdated spreadsheet.
sentences.zh: 有很多想法，但其中一个集中于我们遇到的一个恼人的问题 - 有人需要检查我们的直接和间接竞争对手的网站，提出最重要的变化，并维护那个庞大且总是过时的电子表格。

blocks.html_en: What an amazing job for an AI! And it should be easy. Famous last words.

sentences.en: What an amazing job for an AI!
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能来说，这是多么了不起的工作啊！

sentences.en: And it should be easy.
sentences.zh: 这应该很容易。

sentences.en: Famous last words.
sentences.zh: 著名的遗言。

blocks.html_en: After months of working on this, among other AI experiments, and with multiple people who worked on it, the results were disappointing. The tool worked, but it was picking up too much noise, and filtering out the noise is literally the main job it needs to do perfectly. Eventually, we closed the “R&D department,” and almost abandoned the idea.

sentences.en: After months of working on this, among other AI experiments, and with multiple people who worked on it, the results were disappointing.
sentences.zh: 经过几个月的研究以及其他人工智能实验以及多人的研究，结果令人失望。

sentences.en: The tool worked, but it was picking up too much noise, and filtering out the noise is literally the main job it needs to do perfectly.
sentences.zh: 该工具有效，但它拾取了太多噪音，而滤除噪音实际上是它需要完美完成的主要工作。

sentences.en: Eventually, we closed the “R&D department,” and almost abandoned the idea.
sentences.zh: 最终，我们关闭了“研发部门”，几乎放弃了这个想法。

blocks.html_en: Luckily, someone introduced us to a team of young, smart people who focused solely on AI. They said they could build this tool, and they actually delivered in less than 2 months. We decided to focus on its core function and remove everything nonessential. But we had a working version in September 2025.

sentences.en: Luckily, someone introduced us to a team of young, smart people who focused solely on AI.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，有人向我们介绍了一个由年轻、聪明的人组成的团队，他们只专注于人工智能。

sentences.en: They said they could build this tool, and they actually delivered in less than 2 months.
sentences.zh: 他们说他们可以构建这个工具，实际上他们在不到 2 个月的时间内就交付了。

sentences.en: We decided to focus on its core function and remove everything nonessential.
sentences.zh: 我们决定专注于其核心功能并删除所有不必要的内容。

sentences.en: But we had a working version in September 2025.
sentences.zh: 但我们在 2025 年 9 月就有了一个工作版本。

blocks.html_en: The key part of the app was an email that you receive every Monday. It’s a tailored “newsletter” that tells you about all the key changes your competitors made over the past 7 days. They even added a brilliant feature - when you add competitors, they try to get an older version of their website from the Internet Wayback Machine so you can see a preview without waiting a week or longer. We set up Vacation Tracker’s competitors and invited a few friends to test it out. Then we almost forgot about it.

sentences.en: The key part of the app was an email that you receive every Monday.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序的关键部分是您每周一收到的一封电子邮件。

sentences.en: It’s a tailored “newsletter” that tells you about all the key changes your competitors made over the past 7 days.
sentences.zh: 这是一份量身定制的“新闻通讯”，告诉您竞争对手在过去 7 天内所做的所有关键变化。

sentences.en: They even added a brilliant feature - when you add competitors, they try to get an older version of their website from the Internet Wayback Machine so you can see a preview without waiting a week or longer.
sentences.zh: 他们甚至添加了一个出色的功能 - 当您添加竞争对手时，他们会尝试从互联网 Wayback Machine 获取其网站的旧版本，这样您就可以看到预览，而无需等待一周或更长时间。

sentences.en: We set up Vacation Tracker’s competitors and invited a few friends to test it out.
sentences.zh: 我们设置了 Vacation Tracker 的竞争对手并邀请了一些朋友来测试它。

sentences.en: Then we almost forgot about it.
sentences.zh: 然后我们就几乎忘记了这件事。

blocks.html_en: As time passed, priorities pushed the new product down the list. But we continued reading these Monday emails. Eventually, Lav and I managed to free up some time and decided to build some new products and retest the idea that we can build and maintain a product with AI alone. We needed a playground, and Competitor Tracker was a perfect one.

sentences.en: As time passed, priorities pushed the new product down the list.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，优先事项将新产品推到了列表的后面。

sentences.en: But we continued reading these Monday emails.
sentences.zh: 但我们继续阅读这些周一的电子邮件。

sentences.en: Eventually, Lav and I managed to free up some time and decided to build some new products and retest the idea that we can build and maintain a product with AI alone.
sentences.zh: 最终，Lav 和我设法腾出了一些时间，决定构建一些新产品，并重新测试我们可以仅用人工智能构建和维护产品的想法。

sentences.en: We needed a playground, and Competitor Tracker was a perfect one.
sentences.zh: 我们需要一个游乐场，而竞争对手追踪器是一个完美的游乐场。

blocks.html_en: It took longer than I expected. Two months from the first prompt to a fully working product with a website that follows the brand voice and style and is indexed. The main problems that slowed us down are time, numerous unexpected roadblocks (crawling, various errors, timeouts, noise, etc.), and the use of Cloudflare, a platform I’m less familiar with than AWS. More on these choices below.

sentences.en: It took longer than I expected.
sentences.zh: 花费的时间比我预期的要长。

sentences.en: Two months from the first prompt to a fully working product with a website that follows the brand voice and style and is indexed.
sentences.zh: 从第一次提示到完全正常工作的产品以及遵循品牌声音和风格并被索引的网站需要两个月的时间。

sentences.en: The main problems that slowed us down are time, numerous unexpected roadblocks (crawling, various errors, timeouts, noise, etc.), and the use of Cloudflare, a platform I’m less familiar with than AWS.
sentences.zh: 拖慢我们速度的主要问题是时间、众多意想不到的障碍（爬行、各种错误、超时、噪音等），以及 Cloudflare 的使用，这是一个我对 AWS 不太熟悉的平台。

sentences.en: More on these choices below.
sentences.zh: 下面详细介绍这些选择。

blocks.html_en: So, is it ready now?

sentences.en: So, is it ready now?
sentences.zh: 那么，现在准备好了吗？

blocks.html_en: Introducing Competitor Tracker

sentences.en: Introducing Competitor Tracker
sentences.zh: 竞争对手追踪器简介

blocks.html_en: We can finally introduce our new product: Competitor Tracker! Competitor Tracker is an application that helps you - track your competitors. We are not very creative with names, obviously. Actually, figuring out the best name is not part of the MVP, at least not in our case. We can figure that out later if needed.

sentences.en: We can finally introduce our new product: Competitor Tracker!
sentences.zh: 我们终于可以介绍我们的新产品：竞争对手追踪器！

sentences.en: Competitor Tracker is an application that helps you - track your competitors.
sentences.zh: 竞争对手跟踪器是一款帮助您跟踪竞争对手的应用程序。

sentences.en: We are not very creative with names, obviously.
sentences.zh: 显然，我们的名字并不是很有创意。

sentences.en: Actually, figuring out the best name is not part of the MVP, at least not in our case.
sentences.zh: 事实上，找出最好的名字并不是 MVP 的一部分，至少在我们的例子中不是。

sentences.en: We can figure that out later if needed.
sentences.zh: 如果需要的话，我们可以稍后再解决。

blocks.html_en: But Competitor Tracker, or, to be on-brand, Competitor Tracker & Co., is more than just another application that tracks competitors. The world changed with AI, so we built this product to be equally human and AI-friendly.

sentences.en: But Competitor Tracker, or, to be on-brand, Competitor Tracker & Co., is more than just another application that tracks competitors.
sentences.zh: 但Competitor Tracker，或者用品牌名称来说，Competitor Tracker & Co.，不仅仅是另一个跟踪竞争对手的应用程序。

sentences.en: The world changed with AI, so we built this product to be equally human and AI-friendly.
sentences.zh: 世界因人工智能而改变，因此我们打造的这款产品既人性化又对人工智能友好。

blocks.html_en: What does that even mean?

sentences.en: What does that even mean?
sentences.zh: 这到底意味着什么？

blocks.html_en: First, everything is an API (with detailed scope-based permissions). You can use the API with OAuth2 or an API key, but you can do absolutely everything using the API. That does not make the product AI-friendly, but if everything starts with an API and detailed documentation, then you are going in the right direction.

sentences.en: First, everything is an API (with detailed scope-based permissions).
sentences.zh: 首先，一切都是 API（具有详细的基于范围的权限）。

sentences.en: You can use the API with OAuth2 or an API key, but you can do absolutely everything using the API.
sentences.zh: 您可以将 API 与 OAuth2 或 API 密钥结合使用，但您绝对可以使用 API 完成所有操作。

sentences.en: That does not make the product AI-friendly, but if everything starts with an API and detailed documentation, then you are going in the right direction.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着该产品对人工智能友好，但如果一切都从 API 和详细文档开始，那么您就朝着正确的方向前进。

blocks.html_en: Then, the web app and MCP use that AI to expose all the functionality to humans and agents. Everything you can do from the web dashboard, your agent can do via MCP. No exceptions.

sentences.en: Then, the web app and MCP use that AI to expose all the functionality to humans and agents.
sentences.zh: 然后，Web 应用程序和 MCP 使用该 AI 向人类和代理公开所有功能。

sentences.en: Everything you can do from the web dashboard, your agent can do via MCP.
sentences.zh: 您可以从 Web 仪表板执行的所有操作，您的代理都可以通过 MCP 执行。

sentences.en: No exceptions.
sentences.zh: 没有例外。

blocks.html_en: Competitor Tracker sends you a digest email every Monday. But agents get a webhook, so you do not need to give them access to your inbox. Same data, same filters, same time.

sentences.en: Competitor Tracker sends you a digest email every Monday.
sentences.zh: 竞争对手跟踪器每周一向您发送一封摘要电子邮件。

sentences.en: But agents get a webhook, so you do not need to give them access to your inbox.
sentences.zh: 但代理可以获得网络挂钩，因此您无需授予他们访问您收件箱的权限。

sentences.en: Same data, same filters, same time.
sentences.zh: 相同的数据、相同的过滤器、相同的时间。

blocks.html_en: Finally, the website is built for humans and agents. We serve markdown for agents and HTML for humans. If you want to test it out, there’s a switch at the bottom of every page that helps you enter the Matrix. Pardon, it helps you see the “AI agent” view.

sentences.en: Finally, the website is built for humans and agents.
sentences.zh: 最后，该网站是为人类和代理构建的。

sentences.en: We serve markdown for agents and HTML for humans.
sentences.zh: 我们为代理提供 Markdown，为人类提供 HTML。

sentences.en: If you want to test it out, there’s a switch at the bottom of every page that helps you enter the Matrix.
sentences.zh: 如果你想测试一下，每个页面底部都有一个开关可以帮助你进入矩阵。

sentences.en: Pardon, it helps you see the “AI agent” view.
sentences.zh: 抱歉，它可以帮助您查看“AI 代理”视图。

blocks.html_en: Besides humans and agents, we also have a dog - C. T. Lucky is our lead detective who guides you through our new noir platform and sends you Monday “dossiers.” It’s an AI dog, but still a good one.

sentences.en: Besides humans and agents, we also have a dog - C.
sentences.zh: 除了人类和特工之外，我们还有一只狗——C.

sentences.en: T.
sentences.zh: T。

sentences.en: Lucky is our lead detective who guides you through our new noir platform and sends you Monday “dossiers.”
sentences.zh: 幸运的是我们的首席侦探，他引导您使用我们新的黑色平台，并在周一向您发送“档案”。

sentences.en: It’s an AI dog, but still a good one.
sentences.zh: 这是一只人工智能狗，但仍然是一只好狗。

blocks.html_en: So, it’s time - meet Competitor Tracker & Co.: the AI agency whose pack of agents tails your competitors’ public pages all week and files one short, ranked dossier every Monday, so your team reads the moves that matter instead of babysitting another alert feed - https://competitortracker.io.

sentences.en: So, it’s time - meet Competitor Tracker & Co.: the AI agency whose pack of agents tails your competitors’ public pages all week and files one short, ranked dossier every Monday, so your team reads the moves that matter instead of babysitting another alert feed - https://competitortracker.io.
sentences.zh: 所以，是时候认识一下 Competitor Tracker & Co.：这家人工智能机构，其代理团队整周跟踪竞争对手的公共页面，并每周一提交一份简短的排名档案，这样您的团队就可以阅读重要的举动，而不是照顾另一个警报源 - https://competitortracker.io。

blocks.html_en: If you want to meet C. T. Lucky and Competitor Tracker & Co., head to the “What is Competitor Tracker & Co.” page, see what our pack of agents can do, or check out the product demo (for humans and agents) and try it out (it takes a few minutes to set it up, and the first 25 coins are on the house).

sentences.en: If you want to meet C.
sentences.zh: 如果你想认识C.

sentences.en: T.
sentences.zh: T。

sentences.en: Lucky and Competitor Tracker & Co., head to the “What is Competitor Tracker & Co.”
sentences.zh: Lucky 和 ​​Competitor Tracker & Co.，前往“什么是 Competitor Tracker & Co.”

sentences.en: page, see what our pack of agents can do, or check out the product demo (for humans and agents) and try it out (it takes a few minutes to set it up, and the first 25 coins are on the house).
sentences.zh: 页面，看看我们的代理包可以做什么，或者查看产品演示（针对人类和代理）并尝试一下（设置需要几分钟，前 25 个硬币就在房子里）。

blocks.html_en: Building Competitor Tracker

sentences.en: Building Competitor Tracker
sentences.zh: 建立竞争对手追踪器

blocks.html_en: I saw an amazing tweet yesterday: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381.

sentences.en: I saw an amazing tweet yesterday: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381.
sentences.zh: 我昨天看到一条很棒的推文：https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381。

blocks.html_en: It’s almost perfectly aligned with my experience building products with AI so far. You need an idea and a few hours for a breathtaking demo. Then you need months to finish the last 10%. Of course, that’s not a real 10%, it’s an illusion that tricks you into thinking you can do everything almost instantly.

sentences.en: It’s almost perfectly aligned with my experience building products with AI so far.
sentences.zh: 这与我迄今为止使用人工智能构建产品的经验几乎完全一致。

sentences.en: You need an idea and a few hours for a breathtaking demo.
sentences.zh: 您需要一个想法和几个小时来进行令人惊叹的演示。

sentences.en: Then you need months to finish the last 10%.
sentences.zh: 那么你需要几个月的时间才能完成最后的 10%。

sentences.en: Of course, that’s not a real 10%, it’s an illusion that tricks you into thinking you can do everything almost instantly.
sentences.zh: 当然，这并不是真正的 10%，而是一种幻觉，让你认为自己几乎可以立即完成所有事情。

blocks.html_en: I would go a step further and say that you need a few hours for a demo, a few weeks to implement 200% of the code, and then months to clean up all the AI slop and replace a small part of it with working features.

sentences.en: I would go a step further and say that you need a few hours for a demo, a few weeks to implement 200% of the code, and then months to clean up all the AI slop and replace a small part of it with working features.
sentences.zh: 我会更进一步说，你需要几个小时进行演示，几周的时间来实现 200% 的代码，然后几个月的时间来清理所有 AI 垃圾并用工作功能替换其中的一小部分。

blocks.html_en: Image based on: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381.

sentences.en: Image based on: https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381.
sentences.zh: 图片基于：https://x.com/krishdotdev/status/2077723982028210381。

blocks.html_en: Something similar happened to Competitor Tracker. But that wasn’t the fun part of the journey.

sentences.en: Something similar happened to Competitor Tracker.
sentences.zh: 类似的情况也发生在竞争对手追踪器上。

sentences.en: But that wasn’t the fun part of the journey.
sentences.zh: 但这并不是旅程中有趣的部分。

blocks.html_en: Personally, the fun part was setting everything up.

sentences.en: Personally, the fun part was setting everything up.
sentences.zh: 就个人而言，有趣的部分是设置一切。

blocks.html_en: And everything started with a brand decision. I tried out a few styles, and the film noir one looked like the best fit. At the same time, I asked Claude Code to prepare the initial set of documents based on the prototype source code.

sentences.en: And everything started with a brand decision.
sentences.zh: 一切都始于品牌决策。

sentences.en: I tried out a few styles, and the film noir one looked like the best fit.
sentences.zh: 我尝试了几种风格，黑色电影风格看起来是最合适的。

sentences.en: At the same time, I asked Claude Code to prepare the initial set of documents based on the prototype source code.
sentences.zh: 同时，我要求Claude Code根据原型源代码准备最初的一套文档。

blocks.html_en: Then, because I know that Lav will connect his Hermes and OpenClaw agents to the repo, I decided to separate the product and marketing website repositories. Don’t get me wrong, I like both Hermes and OpenClaw, but I do not trust any AI agent with the full set of permissions.

sentences.en: Then, because I know that Lav will connect his Hermes and OpenClaw agents to the repo, I decided to separate the product and marketing website repositories.
sentences.zh: 然后，因为我知道 Lav 会将他的 Hermes 和 OpenClaw 代理连接到存储库，所以我决定将产品和营销网站存储库分开。

sentences.en: Don’t get me wrong, I like both Hermes and OpenClaw, but I do not trust any AI agent with the full set of permissions.
sentences.zh: 不要误会我的意思，我喜欢 Hermes 和 OpenClaw，但我不信任任何拥有全套权限的 AI 代理。

blocks.html_en: The next step was picking up the platform. AWS was an obvious choice, as I know it well enough. But obvious choices are not fun. To make the process more fun and learn something new, I picked Cloudflare instead.

sentences.en: The next step was picking up the platform.
sentences.zh: 下一步是拿起平台。

sentences.en: AWS was an obvious choice, as I know it well enough.
sentences.zh: AWS 是一个显而易见的选择，我对此非常了解。

sentences.en: But obvious choices are not fun.
sentences.zh: 但显而易见的选择并不有趣。

sentences.en: To make the process more fun and learn something new, I picked Cloudflare instead.
sentences.zh: 为了让这个过程更有趣并学习新东西，我选择了 Cloudflare。

blocks.html_en: Why Cloudflare? I wanted to try it out for a long time. I know a few smart people who work there, and some other smart people I respect praised it. That was already a good excuse to try it out. However, there’s also another, probably even more important perspective: AWS was too low-level for this application, and Cloudflare has most of the critical pieces as services: Workers for compute, Browser Run for scraping, AI Gateway for accessing AI agents, R2 for file storage, and Email service for sending weekly emails. All boxes checked. The best of all was being able to calculate the exact cost of the system on a single platform and decide on the pricing for Competitor Tracker.

sentences.en: Why Cloudflare?
sentences.zh: 为什么选择 Cloudflare？

sentences.en: I wanted to try it out for a long time.
sentences.zh: 我想尝试一下很久了。

sentences.en: I know a few smart people who work there, and some other smart people I respect praised it.
sentences.zh: 我认识一些在那里工作的聪明人，我尊敬的其他一些聪明人也称赞了它。

sentences.en: That was already a good excuse to try it out.
sentences.zh: 这已经是尝试一下的好借口了。

sentences.en: However, there’s also another, probably even more important perspective: AWS was too low-level for this application, and Cloudflare has most of the critical pieces as services: Workers for compute, Browser Run for scraping, AI Gateway for accessing AI agents, R2 for file storage, and Email service for sending weekly emails.
sentences.zh: 然而，还有另一个可能更重要的观点：对于这个应用程序来说，AWS 的级别太低，而 Cloudflare 拥有大多数关键部分作为服务：用于计算的 Workers、用于抓取的 Browser Run、用于访问 AI 代理的 AI Gateway、用于文件存储的 R2 以及用于每周发送电子邮件的电子邮件服务。

sentences.en: All boxes checked.
sentences.zh: 所有方框均已选中。

sentences.en: The best of all was being able to calculate the exact cost of the system on a single platform and decide on the pricing for Competitor Tracker.
sentences.zh: 最棒的是能够在单一平台上计算系统的准确成本并决定竞争对手跟踪器的定价。

blocks.html_en: Cloudflare has its downsides, too. AWS is superior in many areas, including fine-grained permissions, monitoring, sub-accounts (staging, etc.), and more. E.g., AWS is a way better platform for our other product - Vacation Tracker. But Cloudflare still seems like a perfect fit for Competitor Tracker.

sentences.en: Cloudflare has its downsides, too.
sentences.zh: Cloudflare 也有其缺点。

sentences.en: AWS is superior in many areas, including fine-grained permissions, monitoring, sub-accounts (staging, etc.), and more.
sentences.zh: AWS在很多方面都比较优越，包括细粒度的权限、监控、子账户（staging等）等等。

sentences.en: E.g., AWS is a way better platform for our other product - Vacation Tracker.
sentences.zh: 例如，对于我们的其他产品——假期追踪器来说，AWS 是一个更好的平台。

sentences.en: But Cloudflare still seems like a perfect fit for Competitor Tracker.
sentences.zh: 但 Cloudflare 似乎仍然非常适合竞争对手跟踪器。

blocks.html_en: After selecting the platform, setting up tooling, a CI/CD pipeline, and a staging environment, and configuring all critical verifications and tests, it was time to onboard Lav’s agents.

sentences.en: After selecting the platform, setting up tooling, a CI/CD pipeline, and a staging environment, and configuring all critical verifications and tests, it was time to onboard Lav’s agents.
sentences.zh: 选择平台、设置工具、CI/CD 管道和临时环境，并配置所有关键验证和测试后，是时候加入 Lav 的代理了。

blocks.html_en: And then, the magic started happening. Claude Code was in charge of features. Open Code with GLM-5.2 joined as a collaborator (and impressed me as I didn’t expect much). ChatGPT helped with images and to stay on brand. Hermes and OpenClaw started filling in the marketing website’s missing pieces and occasionally even worked on the product.

sentences.en: And then, the magic started happening.
sentences.zh: 然后，奇迹开始发生。

sentences.en: Claude Code was in charge of features.
sentences.zh: 克劳德·科德（Claude Code）负责功能。

sentences.en: Open Code with GLM-5.2 joined as a collaborator (and impressed me as I didn’t expect much).
sentences.zh: Open Code 与 GLM-5.2 作为合作者加入（给我留下了深刻的印象，因为我并没有抱太大期望）。

sentences.en: ChatGPT helped with images and to stay on brand.
sentences.zh: ChatGPT 帮助塑造形象并保持品牌形象。

sentences.en: Hermes and OpenClaw started filling in the marketing website’s missing pieces and occasionally even worked on the product.
sentences.zh: Hermes 和 OpenClaw 开始填补营销网站的缺失部分，有时甚至开发产品。

blocks.html_en: We finally migrated all the existing users to a new platform almost a month ago. And we had our first sign-up from someone we do not know! We also invited a few more friends, gathered some feedback, and made a few improvements to the platform. Then, we went on vacation. Well, it’s important to rest, probably even more important now with AI, because you can accomplish more when you are focused.

sentences.en: We finally migrated all the existing users to a new platform almost a month ago.
sentences.zh: 大约一个月前，我们终于将所有现有用户迁移到了新平台。

sentences.en: And we had our first sign-up from someone we do not know!
sentences.zh: 我们的第一个注册是来自一个我们不认识的人！

sentences.en: We also invited a few more friends, gathered some feedback, and made a few improvements to the platform.
sentences.zh: 我们还邀请了更多的朋友，收集了一些反馈，并对平台进行了一些改进。

sentences.en: Then, we went on vacation.
sentences.zh: 然后，我们就去度假了。

sentences.en: Well, it’s important to rest, probably even more important now with AI, because you can accomplish more when you are focused.
sentences.zh: 嗯，休息很重要，对于人工智能来说，现在可能更重要，因为当你专注时，你可以完成更多的事情。

blocks.html_en: But now that vacation is over, it’s time to continue. The product seems stable enough. A limited number of users are onboarded, and we already track more than 2,000 companies! The goal is to track more than 130 industries and build quarterly industry overview pages. You can see the first ~30 of them on our website. It’s time to focus on the marketing. But before we do that, let’s talk about the architecture and how we set everything up.

sentences.en: But now that vacation is over, it’s time to continue.
sentences.zh: 但现在假期结束了，是时候继续了。

sentences.en: The product seems stable enough.
sentences.zh: 该产品看起来足够稳定。

sentences.en: A limited number of users are onboarded, and we already track more than 2,000 companies!
sentences.zh: 加入的用户数量有限，但我们已经跟踪了 2,000 多家公司！

sentences.en: The goal is to track more than 130 industries and build quarterly industry overview pages.
sentences.zh: 目标是跟踪 130 多个行业并建立季度行业概览页面。

sentences.en: You can see the first ~30 of them on our website.
sentences.zh: 您可以在我们的网站上看到其中的前 30 个。

sentences.en: It’s time to focus on the marketing.
sentences.zh: 是时候专注于营销了。

sentences.en: But before we do that, let’s talk about the architecture and how we set everything up.
sentences.zh: 但在此之前，让我们先讨论一下架构以及我们如何设置一切。

blocks.html_en: Architecture

sentences.en: Architecture
sentences.zh: 建筑学

blocks.html_en: As I already explained, we picked Cloudflare this time. We could use a mix of AWS and Cloudflare to get the best of both worlds, but I prefer having a single platform for most of the infrastructure. You can call that vendor lock-in if you’d like (but our AWS bill of 1-1.5% of Vacation Tracker’s MRR after 8 years does not really care), or a single point of failure (but when Cloudflare or AWS are down, you probably have more significant problems than not being able to access the Competitor Tracker API). Still, these are all tradeoffs that we are willing to make. You can also argue that this could be a much simpler app on a Hetzner instance, but I still have PTSD from years of figuring out why the app crashed at 10 PM, and this is also more fun anyway.

sentences.en: As I already explained, we picked Cloudflare this time.
sentences.zh: 正如我已经解释过的，我们这次选择了 Cloudflare。

sentences.en: We could use a mix of AWS and Cloudflare to get the best of both worlds, but I prefer having a single platform for most of the infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 我们可以混合使用 AWS 和 Cloudflare 来获得两全其美的效果，但我更喜欢为大多数基础设施使用单一平台。

sentences.en: You can call that vendor lock-in if you’d like (but our AWS bill of 1-1.5% of Vacation Tracker’s MRR after 8 years does not really care), or a single point of failure (but when Cloudflare or AWS are down, you probably have more significant problems than not being able to access the Competitor Tracker API).
sentences.zh: 如果您愿意，您可以将该供应商锁定称为“供应商锁定”（但我们的 AWS 账单在 8 年后占 Vacation Tracker MRR 的 1-1.5%，并不真正关心），也可以称为单点故障（但当 Cloudflare 或 AWS 宕机时，您可能会遇到比无法访问竞争对手 Tracker API 更严重的问题）。

sentences.en: Still, these are all tradeoffs that we are willing to make.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，这些都是我们愿意做出的权衡。

sentences.en: You can also argue that this could be a much simpler app on a Hetzner instance, but I still have PTSD from years of figuring out why the app crashed at 10 PM, and this is also more fun anyway.
sentences.zh: 您也可以争辩说，这可能是 Hetzner 实例上的一个更简单的应用程序，但多年来我一直在弄清楚为什么该应用程序在晚上 10 点崩溃，所以我仍然患有 PTSD，而且无论如何，这也更有趣。

blocks.html_en: An oversimplified architecture would look like the following diagram.

sentences.en: An oversimplified architecture would look like the following diagram.
sentences.zh: 过于简化的架构如下图所示。

blocks.html_en: In short:

sentences.en: In short:
sentences.zh: 简而言之：

blocks.html_en: • The Competitor Tracker app treats humans and AI agents as equal users.

sentences.en: The Competitor Tracker app treats humans and AI agents as equal users.
sentences.zh: 竞争对手跟踪器应用程序将人类和人工智能代理视为平等的用户。

blocks.html_en: • Humans can use either the web dashboard or an API. Or they can simply talk to their favorite AI agents.

sentences.en: Humans can use either the web dashboard or an API.
sentences.zh: 人们可以使用网络仪表板或 API。

sentences.en: Or they can simply talk to their favorite AI agents.
sentences.zh: 或者他们可以简单地与他们最喜欢的人工智能代理交谈。

blocks.html_en: • AI Agents use the API or the MCP.

sentences.en: AI Agents use the API or the MCP.
sentences.zh: AI 代理使用 API 或 MCP。

blocks.html_en: • Our marketing website is created for both humans and agents. We serve HTML to humans and Markdown to AI agents (based on the browser user agent). You can also test both views by switching from the “In person” (human) view to the “By wire” (AI agent) view.

sentences.en: Our marketing website is created for both humans and agents.
sentences.zh: 我们的营销网站是为人类和代理商创建的。

sentences.en: We serve HTML to humans and Markdown to AI agents (based on the browser user agent).
sentences.zh: 我们向人类提供 HTML，向 AI 代理提供 Markdown（基于浏览器用户代理）。

sentences.en: You can also test both views by switching from the “In person” (human) view to the “By wire” (AI agent) view.
sentences.zh: 您还可以通过从“亲自”（人类）视图切换到“通过线”（AI 代理）视图来测试这两个视图。

blocks.html_en: • The Web dashboard is a React application served via Cloudflare Pages. There’s nothing especially interesting there.

sentences.en: The Web dashboard is a React application served via Cloudflare Pages.
sentences.zh: Web 仪表板是一个通过 Cloudflare Pages 提供服务的 React 应用程序。

sentences.en: There’s nothing especially interesting there.
sentences.zh: 那里没有什么特别有趣的。

blocks.html_en: • MCP is a Node.js application that runs in a Cloudflare Worker. It supports all auth types (OAuth2 and API key) and all cool things we found.

sentences.en: MCP is a Node.js application that runs in a Cloudflare Worker.
sentences.zh: MCP 是一个在 Cloudflare Worker 中运行的 Node.js 应用程序。

sentences.en: It supports all auth types (OAuth2 and API key) and all cool things we found.
sentences.zh: 它支持所有身份验证类型（OAuth2 和 API 密钥）以及我们发现的所有很酷的东西。

blocks.html_en: • Both the web dashboard and MCP use the Competitor Tracker API. Everything you do in Competitor Tracker you can do using our API. The public one.

sentences.en: Both the web dashboard and MCP use the Competitor Tracker API.
sentences.zh: Web 仪表板和 MCP 都使用竞争对手跟踪器 API。

sentences.en: Everything you do in Competitor Tracker you can do using our API.
sentences.zh: 您在竞争对手追踪器中所做的一切都可以使用我们的 API 完成。

sentences.en: The public one.
sentences.zh: 公共的一.

blocks.html_en: • We also run multiple AI agents that work on a marketing website and use the API to fill in the data. Of course, agents work on the app, too. I personally wrote up to 3 lines of code. It was faster than explaining to my coding agent what to change.

sentences.en: We also run multiple AI agents that work on a marketing website and use the API to fill in the data.
sentences.zh: 我们还运行多个在营销网站上运行的人工智能代理，并使用 API 来填充数据。

sentences.en: Of course, agents work on the app, too.
sentences.zh: 当然，代理也可以在该应用程序上工作。

sentences.en: I personally wrote up to 3 lines of code.
sentences.zh: 我个人最多写了3行代码。

sentences.en: It was faster than explaining to my coding agent what to change.
sentences.zh: 这比向我的编码代理解释要更改什么要快。

blocks.html_en: • API is another Node.js app in a Cloudflare Worker. It uses the OpenAPI spec, which also generates up-to-date API docs with each deployment. The API docs worker is not in the diagram, but it uses fumadocs, and it’s built as a static app (I am not a fan of SSR React apps). The API requires either a bearer token or an API key.

sentences.en: API is another Node.js app in a Cloudflare Worker.
sentences.zh: API 是 Cloudflare Worker 中的另一个 Node.js 应用程序。

sentences.en: It uses the OpenAPI spec, which also generates up-to-date API docs with each deployment.
sentences.zh: 它使用 OpenAPI 规范，该规范还会在每次部署时生成最新的 API 文档。

sentences.en: The API docs worker is not in the diagram, but it uses fumadocs, and it’s built as a static app (I am not a fan of SSR React apps).
sentences.zh: API 文档工作器不在图中，但它使用 fumadocs，并且它是作为静态应用程序构建的（我不是 SSR React 应用程序的粉丝）。

sentences.en: The API requires either a bearer token or an API key.
sentences.zh: API 需要不记名令牌或 API 密钥。

blocks.html_en: • A dedicated auth worker is the identity provider for all apps: a self-hosted OAuth 2.0/OIDC server with passwordless email login and PKCE-secured authorization-code flow, issuing short-lived ES256 JWTs with rotating refresh tokens. It supports Dynamic Client Registration and standard discovery, so clients like the MCP server can register themselves and validate tokens against its JWKS with zero manual setup.

sentences.en: A dedicated auth worker is the identity provider for all apps: a self-hosted OAuth 2.0/OIDC server with passwordless email login and PKCE-secured authorization-code flow, issuing short-lived ES256 JWTs with rotating refresh tokens.
sentences.zh: 专用的身份验证工作人员是所有应用程序的身份提供者：一个自托管的 OAuth 2.0/OIDC 服务器，具有无密码电子邮件登录和 PKCE 保护的授权代码流，发布具有轮换刷新令牌的短期 ES256 JWT。

sentences.en: It supports Dynamic Client Registration and standard discovery, so clients like the MCP server can register themselves and validate tokens against its JWKS with zero manual setup.
sentences.zh: 它支持动态客户端注册和标准发现，因此像 MCP 服务器这样的客户端可以自行注册并根据其 JWKS 验证令牌，并且零手动设置。

blocks.html_en: • The core of the system is another Cloudflare Worker that hosts the scraper. The scraper runs on a schedule and uses Cloudflare Browser Run and a few backup systems to convert all websites into HTML snapshots, screenshots, and markdown files. This is the most complex part of the system, as it handles the business logic, AI interactions, data storage, and all workflows with retries and self-recovery mechanisms.

sentences.en: The core of the system is another Cloudflare Worker that hosts the scraper.
sentences.zh: 该系统的核心是另一个托管抓取工具的 Cloudflare Worker。

sentences.en: The scraper runs on a schedule and uses Cloudflare Browser Run and a few backup systems to convert all websites into HTML snapshots, screenshots, and markdown files.
sentences.zh: 该抓取工具按计划运行，并使用 Cloudflare Browser Run 和一些备份系统将所有网站转换为 HTML 快照、屏幕截图和 Markdown 文件。

sentences.en: This is the most complex part of the system, as it handles the business logic, AI interactions, data storage, and all workflows with retries and self-recovery mechanisms.
sentences.zh: 这是系统中最复杂的部分，因为它处理业务逻辑、人工智能交互、数据存储以及具有重试和自我恢复机制的所有工作流程。

blocks.html_en: • As already mentioned, the scraper uses multiple AI models to detect changes and filter out the noise. We decided to use the Cloudflare AI Gateway for everything so we can easily calculate the cost of each competitor we track.

sentences.en: As already mentioned, the scraper uses multiple AI models to detect changes and filter out the noise.
sentences.zh: 正如已经提到的，抓取工具使用多个人工智能模型来检测变化并过滤掉噪音。

sentences.en: We decided to use the Cloudflare AI Gateway for everything so we can easily calculate the cost of each competitor we track.
sentences.zh: 我们决定对所有事情都使用 Cloudflare AI Gateway，这样我们就可以轻松计算我们跟踪的每个竞争对手的成本。

blocks.html_en: • All pages (HTML and markdown files, and screenshots) are stored in the archive (Cloudflare R2).

sentences.en: All pages (HTML and markdown files, and screenshots) are stored in the archive (Cloudflare R2).
sentences.zh: 所有页面（HTML 和 Markdown 文件以及屏幕截图）都存储在存档 (Cloudflare R2) 中。

blocks.html_en: • All changes and metadata are stored in the SQLite database (D1).

sentences.en: All changes and metadata are stored in the SQLite database (D1).
sentences.zh: 所有更改和元数据都存储在 SQLite 数据库 (D1) 中。

blocks.html_en: • Each Monday, another Cloudflare worker uses AI (via AI Gateway) to prepare Monday dossiers for humans and agents.

sentences.en: Each Monday, another Cloudflare worker uses AI (via AI Gateway) to prepare Monday dossiers for humans and agents.
sentences.zh: 每个星期一，另一名 Cloudflare 工作人员都会使用 AI（通过 AI Gateway）为人类和代理准备星期一的档案。

blocks.html_en: • Once the dossier is ready, humans receive emails on Monday morning (via Cloudflare Email service).

sentences.en: Once the dossier is ready, humans receive emails on Monday morning (via Cloudflare Email service).
sentences.zh: 档案准备好后，人们会在周一早上收到电子邮件（通过 Cloudflare 电子邮件服务）。

blocks.html_en: • At the same time, your AI agents receive the same dossier via webhook event.

sentences.en: At the same time, your AI agents receive the same dossier via webhook event.
sentences.zh: 同时，您的 AI 代理通过 Webhook 事件接收相同的档案。

blocks.html_en: Of course, there’s much more than this. We needed observability, alerts, fallbacks, Slack integration, CI/CD, multiple coding agents, etc.

sentences.en: Of course, there’s much more than this.
sentences.zh: 当然，还有更多。

sentences.en: We needed observability, alerts, fallbacks, Slack integration, CI/CD, multiple coding agents, etc.
sentences.zh: 我们需要可观察性、警报、回退、Slack 集成、CI/CD、多个编码代理等。

blocks.html_en: How things work

sentences.en: How things work
sentences.zh: 事情是如何运作的

blocks.html_en: The idea is simple: outsource as much as possible to AI agents while still maintaining control over output quality. Or, to get back to our bottlenecks story from the beginning of this article: optimize the flow around our time, focus, and taste to maximize that local bottleneck as much as possible.

sentences.en: The idea is simple: outsource as much as possible to AI agents while still maintaining control over output quality.
sentences.zh: 这个想法很简单：尽可能多地外包给人工智能代理，同时仍然保持对输出质量的控制。

sentences.en: Or, to get back to our bottlenecks story from the beginning of this article: optimize the flow around our time, focus, and taste to maximize that local bottleneck as much as possible.
sentences.zh: 或者，回到本文开头的瓶颈故事：优化我们的时间、焦点和品味的流程，以尽可能地最大化局部瓶颈。

blocks.html_en: What does that mean in practice? Many things, but the most important is the ability to verify the output of all our AI agents (coding and others) as much as possible before we review it. Ok, that seems logical, but how do you do that? By adding another LLM to check the output?

sentences.en: What does that mean in practice?
sentences.zh: 这在实践中意味着什么？

sentences.en: Many things, but the most important is the ability to verify the output of all our AI agents (coding and others) as much as possible before we review it.
sentences.zh: 很多事情，但最重要的是在我们审查之前尽可能验证所有人工智能代理（编码和其他）的输出的能力。

sentences.en: Ok, that seems logical, but how do you do that?
sentences.zh: 好吧，这看起来很合乎逻辑，但是你如何做到这一点呢？

sentences.en: By adding another LLM to check the output?
sentences.zh: 通过添加另一个LLM来检查输出？

blocks.html_en: No, not really. LLMs are not deterministic, which means that asking it the same question 10 times yields (often many) different answers. And that’s the main reason why they feel magical, and it’s also the main reason why you can’t just trust them. However, adding another LLM (even a different one) to verify the first LLM’s output does not fix the problem. It probably helps in many cases, but not in 100% of the cases. And when you know there’s a small chance that it can generate some weird output that can hurt your business, you need to keep your attention on 100% to be able to catch that if or when it happens.

sentences.en: No, not really.
sentences.zh: 不，不是真的。

sentences.en: LLMs are not deterministic, which means that asking it the same question 10 times yields (often many) different answers.
sentences.zh: LLM 不是确定性的，这意味着向其提出同一问题 10 次会产生（通常是多次）不同的答案。

sentences.en: And that’s the main reason why they feel magical, and it’s also the main reason why you can’t just trust them.
sentences.zh: 这就是他们感觉神奇的主要原因，也是你不能信任他们的主要原因。

sentences.en: However, adding another LLM (even a different one) to verify the first LLM’s output does not fix the problem.
sentences.zh: 然而，添加另一个 LLM（甚至是不同的）来验证第一个 LLM 的输出并不能解决问题。

sentences.en: It probably helps in many cases, but not in 100% of the cases.
sentences.zh: 它可能在很多情况下都有帮助，但并不是 100% 的情况下都有帮助。

sentences.en: And when you know there’s a small chance that it can generate some weird output that can hurt your business, you need to keep your attention on 100% to be able to catch that if or when it happens.
sentences.zh: 当您知道它有很小的机会产生一些奇怪的输出，从而损害您的业务时，您需要保持 100% 的注意力，以便能够在它发生时抓住它。

blocks.html_en: However, there’s one powerful tool that we use every day that can convert some of these checks to deterministic ones - a linter. When you write your code, you often have formatters (e.g., prettier, or in our case oxfmt) and linters (e.g., eslint, or in our case oxlint) that format your code and check if it follows the rules. Luckily, most of these formatters and linters are extendable - you can create a JavaScript plugin that checks your specific thing. But writing these plugins is not always easy and pleasant. But, again, luckily, your AI coding agent is quite good at writing these.

sentences.en: However, there’s one powerful tool that we use every day that can convert some of these checks to deterministic ones - a linter.
sentences.zh: 然而，我们每天都使用一种强大的工具，可以将其中一些检查转换为确定性检查 - linter。

sentences.en: When you write your code, you often have formatters (e.g., prettier, or in our case oxfmt) and linters (e.g., eslint, or in our case oxlint) that format your code and check if it follows the rules.
sentences.zh: 当你编写代码时，你通常会使用格式化程序（例如，prettier，或者在我们的例子中为 oxfmt）和 linter（例如，eslint，或者在我们的例子中为 oxlint）来格式化你的代码并检查它是否遵循规则。

sentences.en: Luckily, most of these formatters and linters are extendable - you can create a JavaScript plugin that checks your specific thing.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，大多数格式化程序和 linter 都是可扩展的 - 您可以创建一个 JavaScript 插件来检查您的特定内容。

sentences.en: But writing these plugins is not always easy and pleasant.
sentences.zh: 但编写这些插件并不总是那么容易和愉快。

sentences.en: But, again, luckily, your AI coding agent is quite good at writing these.
sentences.zh: 但幸运的是，您的人工智能编码代理非常擅长编写这些内容。

blocks.html_en: With these deterministic checks, we can verify expected outcomes, such as whether our code follows the architecture guidelines (e.g., hexagonal architecture), whether migrations are destructive (we use deprecate-then-drop), and whether CSP is up to date. However, we also do deterministic checks for less common things, such as the voice of the application (we check for some specific words, and ban them, e.g., “harness,” that one is my favorite), or if our developer documentation leaks the implementation details (e.g., Cloudflare Workers, D1 database, R2, etc.).

sentences.en: With these deterministic checks, we can verify expected outcomes, such as whether our code follows the architecture guidelines (e.g., hexagonal architecture), whether migrations are destructive (we use deprecate-then-drop), and whether CSP is up to date.
sentences.zh: 通过这些确定性检查，我们可以验证预期结果，例如我们的代码是否遵循架构指南（例如六边形架构）、迁移是否具有破坏性（我们使用 deprecate-then-drop）以及 CSP 是否是最新的。

sentences.en: However, we also do deterministic checks for less common things, such as the voice of the application (we check for some specific words, and ban them, e.g., “harness,” that one is my favorite), or if our developer documentation leaks the implementation details (e.g., Cloudflare Workers, D1 database, R2, etc.).
sentences.zh: 然而，我们也会对不太常见的事情进行确定性检查，例如应用程序的声音（我们检查一些特定的单词，并禁止它们，例如“harness”，这是我最喜欢的），或者我们的开发人员文档是否泄漏了实现细节（例如，Cloudflare Workers、D1 数据库、R2 等）。

blocks.html_en: These deterministic checks catch many problematic issues before they reach our review. We also run them as part of the CI/CD pipeline, so we can let agents do more, since they know how to recover.

sentences.en: These deterministic checks catch many problematic issues before they reach our review.
sentences.zh: 这些确定性检查在到达我们的审查之前发现了许多有问题的问题。

sentences.en: We also run them as part of the CI/CD pipeline, so we can let agents do more, since they know how to recover.
sentences.zh: 我们还将它们作为 CI/CD 管道的一部分运行，因此我们可以让代理做更多的事情，因为他们知道如何恢复。

blocks.html_en: On recovery, we also try to make linter errors as descriptive as possible and include the instructions on what to do instead. For example, instead of saying “destructive migrations are not allowed,” we say “stop using X in a prior commit … then land this destructive migration in a later commit,” and, when applicable, tell the agent about the escape hatch.

sentences.en: On recovery, we also try to make linter errors as descriptive as possible and include the instructions on what to do instead.
sentences.zh: 在恢复时，我们还尝试使 linter 错误尽可能具有描述性，并包含有关如何操作的说明。

sentences.en: For example, instead of saying “destructive migrations are not allowed,” we say “stop using X in a prior commit …
sentences.zh: 例如，我们不是说“不允许破坏性迁移”，而是说“在之前的提交中停止使用 X……”

sentences.en: then land this destructive migration in a later commit,” and, when applicable, tell the agent about the escape hatch.
sentences.zh: 然后在以后的提交中进行这种破坏性的迁移”，并且在适用的情况下，告诉代理有关逃生舱口的信息。

blocks.html_en: But why stop there? These error hints are useful for both AI agents and humans, so we also baked them into the product. Each product error also returns the resolution hint.

sentences.en: But why stop there?
sentences.zh: 但为什么要停在那里呢？

sentences.en: These error hints are useful for both AI agents and humans, so we also baked them into the product.
sentences.zh: 这些错误提示对于人工智能代理和人类都很有用，因此我们也将它们融入到产品中。

sentences.en: Each product error also returns the resolution hint.
sentences.zh: 每个产品错误还会返回解决提示。

blocks.html_en: In addition to deterministic checks, we build various tools for our agents. We have tools that gather data and generate specific pages, tools for videos, tools for SEO checks, etc. The goal of these tools is to save our time and ensure that the agents have all the correct information. Then we use our time to review the output and do things agents can’t.

sentences.en: In addition to deterministic checks, we build various tools for our agents.
sentences.zh: 除了确定性检查之外，我们还为代理构建各种工具。

sentences.en: We have tools that gather data and generate specific pages, tools for videos, tools for SEO checks, etc. The goal of these tools is to save our time and ensure that the agents have all the correct information.
sentences.zh: 我们有收集数据和生成特定页面的工具、视频工具、SEO 检查工具等。这些工具的目标是节省我们的时间并确保代理拥有所有正确的信息。

sentences.zh: 然后我们利用时间来审查输出并做代理无法做的事情。

blocks.html_en: Another big benefit in the age of AI agents is having the whole product in a monorepo. Your agent can verify features and capabilities before describing them on the product page, etc. You can also ask them to provide screenshots or even videos as part of the task to visually verify their work before running everything.

sentences.en: Another big benefit in the age of AI agents is having the whole product in a monorepo.
sentences.zh: 人工智能代理时代的另一大好处是将整个产品放在单一存储库中。

sentences.en: Your agent can verify features and capabilities before describing them on the product page, etc. You can also ask them to provide screenshots or even videos as part of the task to visually verify their work before running everything.
sentences.zh: 您的代理可以在产品页面等上描述特性和功能之前验证它们。您还可以要求他们提供屏幕截图甚至视频作为任务的一部分，以便在运行所有内容之前直观地验证他们的工作。

blocks.html_en: Finally, you can also benefit from the specific cron jobs and background tasks, or, as they call them today, loops.

sentences.en: Finally, you can also benefit from the specific cron jobs and background tasks, or, as they call them today, loops.
sentences.zh: 最后，您还可以受益于特定的 cron 作业和后台任务，或者像他们今天所说的那样，循环。

blocks.html_en: For example, if you report the issue via our dashboard or an API (everything is an API, remember), it goes to our Linear board. Once Lav or I review these issues, we add additional instructions and the “autofix” label. Then our AI agents pick these up in the background, fix them, make a pull request, and provide screenshots or videos.

blocks.html_en: We use Linear for issues and an amazing new product called Votito for product feedback and surveys. Votito allows you to score ideas from the idea bank using the Impact x Confidence x Ease (ICE) score by uploading documents such as A/B tests, market data, and usability studies. One of the future experiments is to try to make another background process that checks Votito for all ideas with a score over, e.g., 75, and builds them. We would still need to be in the driver’s seat and provide some of these documents, but it would be more fun.
